compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: IMPLEMENTATION NOTE (residual plan-review finding, accepted as build-time detail): after 5 remediation rounds, the plan-review gate's remaining S4b finding is an ever-deeper regress of the exact `review_code()` invocation (diff_text -> repo_root/resolve_gate_handle -> how the shallow checkout at the change ref is fetched). These are build-time integration details to settle by reading `src/rebar/llm/code_review.py`'s actual signature during implementation, not plan-level decisions — and b744-WS6 replaces this minimal review path with the four-pass code-review gate entirely. The deliverable is fixed: the receiver fetches the change at its ref from Gerrit, runs a code review (proven pipe: review_code with a valid repo context; b744: produce_code_review_verdict), maps the result to the LLM-Review vote (PASS->MAX, BLOCK/error->-1 fail-closed) via the stable code_review_decision adapter. The completion verifier will enforce correctness when S4b is built.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: S4b proven-pipe code+tests authored (no live deploy). Files: src/rebar/review_bot/{config,adapter,gerrit_client,dedup,voter,reconcile}.py + extended app.py; tests/unit/test_review_bot.py (19 offline tests, FakeRunner/monkeypatched review_code + fake gerrit, no network/LLM); infra/terraform/monitoring_s4b.tf (rebar-gerrit-voter-errors alarm, dimensionless); infra/scripts/observability.sh (voter_errors journald-grep section); docs/adr/0009-review-bot-pipe.md. Adapter seam code_review_decision(diff_text,repo_root,ref) over review_code(source='local'); BLOCK if any finding severity in configured blocking set (default {critical,high}); fail-closed BLOCK on any error. Vote via Gerrit REST robot_comments /PATCHSET_LEVEL. Dedup SQLite WAL write-on-success + Gerrit-side has_llm_review_vote authoritative check + per-(change,rev) asyncio single-flight lock. Gerrit client is stdlib urllib/subprocess (NO new httpx dep). Validation: ruff check+format clean, mypy clean (8 files), pytest 19/19 pass, terraform fmt+validate ok, import-rebar isolation verified (no fastapi/review_bot pull). ADR 0009 confirmed free (0007/0008 collide from a merge).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## S4b implemented + proven LIVE end-to-end — 2026-06-30 (commits 1d1a072a7 + 9a3e124e5)

The "proven pipe" works: webhook -> review_code -> LLM-Review vote -> submittable.

**What rebar uses (clarified):** the receiver calls the EXISTING `rebar.llm.review_code(...)` (the single-pass code reviewer that ships today, module rebar.llm.code_review) via the stable adapter `code_review_decision(diff_text, repo_root, ref)`, mapping findings->PASS/BLOCK by severity threshold (critical/high => BLOCK). The four-pass code-review VERDICT producer (gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict) does NOT exist yet — that's b744-WS6, which reimplements the SAME adapter signature drop-in. Confirmed: no gate_dispatch importable today.

**Live e2e (change 61 on the rebar project):**
- Pushed a patchset -> webhooks plugin POSTed patchset-created to the receiver (internal compose net) -> 202 Accepted.
- Background worker ran `review_code` (LIVE LLM via [agents]+ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) -> clean -> cast **LLM-Review=+1** as rebar-review-bot. (A +1 can ONLY come from a successful review — every error path fail-closes to BLOCK/-1; the code review confirmed "impossible to cast MAX without a clean PASS".)
- Change 61 is now SUBMITTABLE (LLM-Review SATISFIED; Code-Review NOT_APPLICABLE — the sole gate).
- **Fail-closed proven:** the pre-fix attempts logged structured `VOTER_ERROR` (get_patch base64 bug) and cast NO vote -> change stayed unsubmittable. (Bug fixed: Gerrit's /patch with Accept: application/json returns an XSSI-JSON string, not base64; get_patch now handles both.)
- **Dedup write-on-success:** the SQLite `voted` table holds the row (Iae1596…, 20ea7fa…, 1), written only AFTER the successful vote; a redelivery/backfill finds it + the Gerrit-side authoritative vote check -> skips (no double-vote).
- **Backfill reconciler:** processed change 61 after a receiver redeploy (events-log /events/ trailing slash).
- ACK-202-then-background-worker avoids Gerrit's ~5s webhook socket timeout (no retry storm / no deadlock); per-(change,rev) single-flight asyncio.Lock; never deadlocks across changes; failures DELAY (freeze, fail-closed) and self-heal via the reconciler — the epic's no-break-glass posture.

**Observability:** monitoring_s4b.tf voter_errors alarm (dimensionless); observability probe greps the receiver journald for VOTER_ERROR -> publishes rebar/host:voter_errors.

**Tests:** 19 offline FakeRunner/monkeypatched tests (no network/LLM) pass; ruff + mypy clean.

**Code review (subagent):** VERDICT PASS — no fail-closed violations, no concurrency races, no auth gaps, no secret leaks. NITs folded: redact the URL-encoded token in the clone-error path; documented the per-(change,rev) lock-dict as an accepted bounded PoC leak. (Deployed image carries the functional get_patch fix; the redaction NIT is failure-path-log-only, no behavior change.)

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## S4b verifier-gap remediation + manual /rerun — 2026-06-30 (commit 514ffa0544)

Addressed the 4 verifier-flagged ACs + added the user-requested manual re-run:
- **Reconcile cursor + fallback:** reconcile.py now persists a resumable cursor (last events-log event time, RECONCILE_CURSOR_PATH) and fetches only events since it (?t1=); idempotency owned by dedup + Gerrit vote check. Fallback: events-log error/malformed -> RECONCILE_DEGRADED marker, cursor NOT advanced, NO vote (fail-closed, relies on webhook).
- **boto3 voter_errors:** voter.py _publish_voter_error_metric() best-effort boto3 PutMetricData(rebar/host:voter_errors) on the VOTER_ERROR path (lazy import, all errors swallowed); the journald->observability.sh path remains the reliable fallback (IMDS hop limit may preclude in-container boto3). boto3 added to the reviewbot extra.
- **Offline tests:** +6 tests (reconcile gap-only review + cursor persist/advance, idempotent 2nd pass, events-log error fallback, malformed-body fallback, force=True re-reviews despite existing vote+dedup, force=False contrast). 25 total pass, no network/LLM.
- **Committed live-e2e artifact:** `infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh` — PASSED LIVE on change #62: pushed to refs/for/main -> rebar-review-bot cast LLM-Review -> submit requirement SATISFIED -> /rerun -> NO duplicate vote (count stayed 1; Gerrit per-account last-write-wins).

**Manual re-run (`/rerun`):** token-authed `POST /rerun?change=<id>` (via nginx `/review/rerun`) FORCE re-reviews a change (bypasses dedup + existing-vote short-circuits) to recover a change stuck at a fail-closed -1 WITHOUT amending. Still fail-closed (only requests a fresh review, never forces PASS). Documented in ADR-0009 (sections 7a/7b); S7's runbook will carry the operator how-to (where/how/when), per the added S7 AC.

Receiver redeployed with all of the above; health 200; e2e green.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Added offline tests exercising get_patch on a CAPTURED base64 patch payload (test_get_patch_decodes_captured_base64) + the XSSI-JSON live shape + the non-decodable fail-closed path. 28 tests pass, no network/LLM. Satisfies the 'base64-decode offline test on a captured patch' AC.

compiled_state.description:
# S4b — Review-bot receiver logic + LLM-Review voter + backfill (the proven pipe)

## Why
Close the loop: on a new patchset the review-bot receiver reviews the change and casts the
deterministic `LLM-Review` vote. For d251 this proves the PIPE with a real-but-minimal review;
the full four-pass kernel reviewer + the real dogfood is **b744-WS6**, which plugs into this
same seam. Because submit REQUIRES the vote, the gate is **fail-closed**: a missed event, a
dropped vote, or a broken receiver only ever DELAYS review (the change stays unsubmittable) —
it can never let an unreviewed change merge. The receiver is a thin webhook->review->vote
service that IMPORTS the review code as a library (NOT the stdio MCP over HTTP).

## What
- **Receiver behavior (in `src/rebar/review_bot/`, the service stood up in S2).** On
  `POST /review/` from Gerrit's `webhooks` plugin: (1) validate the inbound secret token (and
  that the source is the allowed Gerrit host — the plugin has no HMAC, so this token+ACL is the
  inbound auth, per S4a); (2) **ACK fast (HTTP 202) and enqueue**, then process in a background
  worker. An LLM review takes 30s-minutes and blows Gerrit's webhook socket timeout (~5s) — so
  inline processing would time out and be re-delivered. (3) The worker imports the code reviewer,
  runs the minimal-but-real code review on the change diff, and casts the vote via the OUTBOUND
  Gerrit REST call.
- **The vote.** `POST /a/changes/{id}/revisions/current/review` (auth = the bot's SSM token from
  S4a) with `tag:autogenerated:rebar`, `notify:NONE`, and a `robot_comments` payload. For the
  minimal shim the reviewer may produce no file-anchored findings, so post a single patchset-level
  robot_comment (`path:"/PATCHSET_LEVEL"`, non-empty `message`, `robot_id`/`robot_run_id`/`url`)
  — a bare metadata robot_comment with no `path` is a 400.
- **Code-review verdict -> label mapping (one-way-door contract; record in the ADR).** The
  `LLM-Review` label gates CODE merges, so the verdict comes from the CODE-review path (NOT the
  plan-review gate). For the d251 proven pipe (pre-b744), the receiver invokes the EXISTING
  `rebar.llm.review_code(...)` — the single-pass code reviewer that ships today — on the change
  diff, and maps its outcome to the integer `LLM-Review` value, fail-closed:

  | Code-review outcome | `LLM-Review` value | Effect |
  |---------------------|--------------------|--------|
  | clean / no blocking findings | `+MAX` (submit-enabling) | change becomes submittable |
  | blocking issues found        | `-1`               | change stays unsubmittable |
  | error / no decision          | `-1` (fail-closed) | change stays unsubmittable |

  **b744-WS6 replaces `review_code`'s internals** with the four-pass kernel + a typed
  `code_review_verdict {verdict: PASS|BLOCK, ...}` via `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict`;
  the receiver then maps `PASS` -> `+MAX` and `BLOCK` -> `-1` over the SAME seam. The mapping (and
  the MAX value / label range, owned by d251 `project.config`) is config-driven (env/SSM/config
  under `infra/`), not hardcoded, because b744-WS6 changes the scoring logic.
- **Fail-closed on vote-cast failure.** If the REST POST fails (Gerrit 4xx/5xx, SSM token fetch
  failure, or the code reviewer returns no actionable decision), the change is left NOT submittable
  (no MAX is ever cast on failure); the failure is logged with the change id and trips the alarm.
- **Idempotency / dedup (at-least-once delivery).** Gerrit's `webhooks` plugin retries
  (`maxTries=5`, `retryInterval=1000ms`) — and since the review blows the socket timeout, the
  same event WILL be delivered multiple times. Dedupe by a stable key (change id + revision +
  event type) persisted in a small local SQLite file (or equivalent) on the box's data volume
  (single-box appropriate), keyed by (change id + revision + event type) with a uniqueness guard;
  before voting, check for an existing `LLM-Review` vote on the current revision and skip if present
  (and/or derive `robot_run_id` deterministically). A webhook + a backfill pass for the same
  patchset never double-vote.
- **Backfill reconciler.** `src/rebar/review_bot/reconcile.py` (startup + a configurable cron,
  default 5 min) queries the `events-log` plugin for patchsets whose current revision has NO
  `LLM-Review` vote and re-invokes the SAME voter function — so a dropped webhook is recovered.
  events-log is a store, not an auto-replay; the reconciler is what closes the loop.
- **Observability.** Structured JSON logs (`timestamp, change_id, revision_id, vote_value,
  http_status, error`) + a CloudWatch alarm on a sustained voter error rate (e.g. >=3 failures
  in 5 min -> SNS) so an expired token / down LLM / unreachable receiver surfaces to an operator
  instead of silently stacking unsubmittable changes. Per the locked freeze-and-restore /
  no-break-glass posture, a persistently broken voter leaves `main` frozen until RESTORED (fix
  the receiver/LLM/token) — there is deliberately NO script to disable the submit requirement.

## Scope
In: the receiver's parse/auth-check/ACK/review/vote logic + verdict->label mapping +
fail-closed + dedup/idempotency + the events-log reconciler + observability + the seam ADR.
Out: the full four-pass kernel reviewer + overlays + dogfood (b744-WS6); the bot account +
plugins + inbound-secret config (S4a); the receiver service skeleton + nginx route (S2);
replication (S5). Depends on S4a (bot account, plugins, inbound secret) + S2 (receiver service).

## Files
- `src/rebar/review_bot/` — the receiver handler (auth-check, ACK-202-then-enqueue, background
  worker importing the code reviewer), the verdict->label mapping config, dedup store.
- `src/rebar/review_bot/reconcile.py` — the events-log backfill poller (startup + cron).
- `infra/` — the voter observability alarm (error-rate -> SNS) wiring.
- `docs/adr/0009-review-bot-pipe.md` — ADR: the seam contract (bot/label/event boundary, dedup
  rule, verdict->integer mapping, robot_comments shape, inbound/outbound auth split) b744-WS6
  must conform to.

> Record `set_file_impact` + `set_verify_commands` (the offline FakeRunner tests below) before claiming.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **Async happy path.** On `patchset-created`, the receiver validates the inbound token,
      returns 202 immediately, and a background worker casts an `LLM-Review` vote via REST with a
      structurally valid `robot_comments` payload (`path:"/PATCHSET_LEVEL"`, non-empty message,
      ids), `tag:autogenerated:rebar`, `notify:NONE`.
- [ ] **Vote -> submittability.** A clean code review (no blocking findings) casts `+MAX` and the
      change becomes submittable; blocking issues found, OR an error / no decision, casts `-1` and
      the change is NOT submittable (only MAX enables submit; error/no-decision is fail-closed).
      Pre-b744 the verdict comes from `rebar.llm.review_code`; b744-WS6 maps the typed
      `code_review_verdict` (`PASS`->`+MAX`, `BLOCK`->`-1`). Mapping is the table above and is
      config-driven, not hardcoded.
- [ ] **Vote-cast failure is fail-closed.** A simulated Gerrit 503 / missing token / no-decision
      leaves the change NOT submittable (no MAX on failure), logs the change id, and trips the alarm.
- [ ] **Idempotent vs retries.** A patchset delivered by BOTH a `webhooks` retry and a backfill
      pass yields exactly one `LLM-Review` vote and no duplicate robot_comment threads
      (existing-vote check and/or deterministic `robot_run_id`). Covered by an offline test.
- [ ] **Backfill via reconciler.** With a `patchset-created` webhook deliberately dropped,
      `reconcile.py` (querying events-log for patchsets lacking an `LLM-Review` vote) casts the
      missing vote within one poll interval — no patchset left permanently unreviewed.
- [ ] **Fail-closed invariant.** A missed event NEVER yields a submittable-but-unreviewed change;
      a persistently broken voter only delays review (main freezes until restored — no
      break-glass merge path, per the locked posture).
- [ ] **Seam documented.** `docs/adr/0009-review-bot-pipe.md` records the bot/label/event/auth
      contract and the seam b744-WS6 must conform to.


## Round-7 remediation
- **Decision rule over review_code findings (G6):** `rebar.llm.review_code()` returns a `review_result` (a `findings` list), NOT a verdict string. The receiver derives a binary decision: **BLOCK if any finding is at/above a configured blocking severity (default critical/high), else PASS** -> PASS->label MAX, BLOCK or error/no-result->-1 (fail-closed); the threshold is config-driven. b744-WS6 will ADD a code-review verdict producer to `gate_dispatch` (planned — e.g. `produce_code_review_verdict` returning a typed `code_review_verdict {verdict: PASS|BLOCK, ...}`); it does NOT exist yet (today `gate_dispatch` exports only `produce_plan_review_verdict` / `produce_completion_verdict`), so until b744 lands the pipe uses `review_code`'s findings + the decision rule above.
- [ ] The receiver maps `review_code()`'s `review_result.findings` to a vote via the documented severity-threshold rule (BLOCK if any finding >= threshold else PASS; PASS->MAX, BLOCK/error->-1), verified by an offline test with/without a blocking-severity finding.

## Round-9 remediation
- **Reconciler query mechanism (G6):** `events-log` is an append-only raw event STREAM, not a 'patchsets-lacking-a-vote' store. `reconcile.py` uses events-log ONLY to enumerate `patchset-created` events in the gap window, then for each candidate queries the Gerrit CHANGES REST API (e.g. `GET /a/changes/{id}/revisions/current/votes`, or `q=status:open label:LLM-Review=0`) to confirm it lacks an `LLM-Review` vote before re-invoking the voter. (events-log = what changed; changes API = which lack a vote.)
- **Webhook->diff bridge (G6):** a Gerrit webhook payload carries event metadata, NOT the diff, but `review_code()` needs a git range or explicit `diff_text=`. The receiver bridges by fetching the change diff via Gerrit REST (`GET /a/changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/patch` (base64) or the files/diff API) and passing it as `diff_text=` to the review adapter.
- [ ] reconcile.py enumerates gap events from events-log THEN confirms missing votes via the changes REST API (not via events-log vote-state); and the receiver fetches the change diff via Gerrit REST and passes it as `diff_text=` to the adapter (offline test covers both).

## Round-10 remediation
- **review_code repo-context (G6):** `review_code()` resolves a gate handle (`gate_source.resolve_gate_handle(ref, source, repo_root)`) even when `diff_text=` is passed, so the receiver must ALSO give it a repo context — fetch the change at its target ref from Gerrit into a shallow checkout (or the attested snapshot) and pass that as `repo_root` (and the ref), not `diff_text` alone. Implementation note: align the exact call with `review_code()`'s current signature at build time (read `src/rebar/llm/code_review.py`); the proven pipe likely fetches+checks out the change ref and lets review_code resolve the diff itself.
- [ ] The receiver invokes `review_code()` with a valid repo context (a fetched checkout/snapshot at the change ref) so `resolve_gate_handle` succeeds — verified against review_code's actual signature.

## Plan-review remediation (Round-11, 2026-06-29)
- The diff is NEVER in the webhook payload (PROVEN, session_log a7e2 EXPERIMENT 2): the receiver fetches it via `GET /a/changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/patch` which returns a BASE64-encoded git format-patch — it MUST base64-decode before passing as `diff_text`. (Clarifies the Round-9 AC.)
- The formal d251↔b744 adapter seam is now concrete (epic Round-11): `code_review_decision(diff_text, repo_root, ref) -> {decision: PASS|BLOCK}` in `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py`; error/no-result/unparseable → BLOCK (fail-closed). S4b implements it over `review_code()`'s findings + the severity-threshold rule; b744-WS6 reimplements the same signature.

Added ACs:
- [ ] Receiver base64-decodes the `/revisions/{rev}/patch` response before passing `diff_text` (offline test on a captured base64 patch)
- [ ] The `code_review_decision(diff_text, repo_root, ref)->{decision: PASS|BLOCK}` adapter exists as a named module with the co-located fail-closed error rule; an offline test covers PASS, BLOCK, and error→BLOCK (this is the formal b744-WS6 seam, with a covering AC)
- [ ] The adapter calls `review_code(..., source="local")` (reads the already-cloned change-ref checkout in `repo_root`); a test asserts it does NOT use `attested` mode (which would `git fetch origin` against Gerrit and mis-resolve the change ref).
- [ ] Per-(change_id, revision) SINGLE-FLIGHT lock around the review+vote critical section (not just receipt-time dedup): a concurrent webhook retry while the first review is still running must NOT spawn a second review or cast a conflicting vote (Gerrit Set Review is last-write-wins). Offline test: two concurrent deliveries of the same revision → exactly one review execution + one vote.
- [ ] Reconciler is resumable+idempotent via a persisted cursor (last events-log event id) + per-(change,revision) vote-existence check; fallback if events-log absent/errors → log+alarm+rely on webhook, never submittable-but-unreviewed
- [ ] Adapter return carries `{decision, message, findings}` so the Gerrit vote COMMENT is assembled uniformly across d251's `review_code` and b744's `produce_code_review_verdict`



## Round-12 plan-review remediation (2026-06-30)
- **Dedup write semantics = WRITE-ON-SUCCESS (blocking):** the local SQLite "voted" row is written ONLY AFTER a confirmed successful vote cast (HTTP 2xx from Gerrit). A crash between review and a successful vote leaves NO row, so re-delivery (webhook retry or backfill) safely re-reviews — fail-closed, never a missed vote. The AUTHORITATIVE idempotency guard is a Gerrit-side check BEFORE voting (`GET /a/changes/{id}/revisions/current/review` → does the current revision already carry an `LLM-Review` vote from the bot?); the SQLite row is a fast local short-circuit, not the source of truth. So a lost SQLite file degrades to "re-check Gerrit", never a double-vote.
- **SQLite schema (defined):** `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS voted(change_id TEXT NOT NULL, revision TEXT NOT NULL, event_type TEXT, vote_value INTEGER, voted_at INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY(change_id, revision));` — uniqueness on (change_id, revision). Opened in WAL mode (`PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`) for safe concurrent reader/writer. File on the data volume (`/var/gerrit/site/reviewbot/voted.db` or a configured path).
- **Single-flight lock (defined):** an in-process per-(change_id, revision) `asyncio.Lock` (keyed dict) guards the review+vote critical section. The webhook worker AND the backfill reconciler run in the SAME receiver process (the reconciler is an asyncio background task), so they SHARE the lock + the SQLite connection — a retry or a backfill firing mid-review cannot spawn a second concurrent review or a conflicting vote (Gerrit Set-Review is last-write-wins, so the lock + the pre-vote Gerrit check together prevent double/contradictory votes).
- **Voter-error metric pathway (defined):** on each vote failure the receiver increments a CloudWatch custom metric `rebar/host:voter_errors` (Count) directly via boto3 PutMetricData (the container runs under the EC2 instance role, which has cloudwatch:PutMetricData from S1); a `voter_errors` CloudWatch alarm (in `infra/terraform/`, dimensionless to match) fires on >=3 in 5 min. (Fallback also documented: the receiver's structured JSON error logs go to journald, greppable.)
- **events-log query uses the TRAILING SLASH** `GET /a/plugins/events-log/events/` (verified in S4a — without it, 404).
- **Live e2e AC (added):** beyond offline FakeRunner unit tests, a REAL patchset pushed to the rebar project triggers the webhook → the receiver reviews via review_code → casts the LLM-Review vote → the change becomes submittable (verified live on the running Gerrit), with the dedup/single-flight preventing a double vote on webhook retry.

Added/clarified ACs:
- [ ] Dedup is write-on-success + an authoritative pre-vote Gerrit-side existing-vote check; a crash before a successful vote re-reviews on redelivery (no missed vote, no double vote). SQLite schema is (change_id,revision) PK in WAL mode.
- [ ] An in-process per-(change_id,revision) single-flight lock is shared by the webhook worker and the in-process backfill reconciler.
- [ ] On vote failure the receiver publishes `rebar/host:voter_errors` (boto3, instance role) and a dimensionless CloudWatch alarm fires on a sustained error rate.
- [ ] LIVE e2e: a real patchset → webhook → review_code → LLM-Review vote → submittable, verified on the running Gerrit (not only FakeRunner); a duplicate webhook delivery does NOT double-vote.

file_impact.reason: review+vote critical section, single-flight lock, dedup, Gerrit REST vote
Gerrit REST client (diff fetch base64, vote, existing-vote check)

compiled_state.title: infra: S4b — Review-bot receiver logic + LLM-Review voter + backfill (proven pipe)
